issues: irregular white pixel scatter in flame core
bracket detail complexity borderline for pixel art
reasoning: 8x zoom inspection shows clean pixel blocks with no sub-pixel blending within individual pixels — good sign for handcrafted art. Outline is consistently 1px dark border around the sprite body. Palette is appropriately limited: white/bright-yellow for flame core, mid-orange for flame body, dark orange-red for flame base, brown-tan for shaft. No smooth gradient shading — transitions are hard-stepped as expected in pixel art. Background is clean black with no noise scatter. Semantic structure is valid: flame top, cylindrical shaft, hook bracket at base. Issues found: (1) the white highlight pixels in the flame core have an irregular scattered distribution that reads slightly inconsistent — not a uniform intentional dither pattern, could indicate AI-generated pixel placement; (2) the flame silhouette is noticeably asymmetric left-right in a way that doesn't follow a clear artistic intent; (3) the bracket/hook at the base has fine detail that is slightly noisy at 8x — a few 1px protrusions that look like melting detail rather than intentional linework. No color bleed detected. No AA smearing on sprite edges. Overall mostly clean pixel art but flame pixel distribution and bracket detail raise mild concerns.
